sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 327 of 2017 ITRAs 328 to 344 of 2017 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of main case. 28.10.2025 Mr. Safdar Ali, advocate holding brief for Mr. Fayaz Ali Metlo, advocate for the applicant. Mr. Anwar Kashif Mumtaz, advocate and Mr. M. Usman Alam, advocate for the respondent. These reference applications are pending since 2017 without any significant progress. Even today brief is being held and adjournment is being sought on behalf of applicant for no cogent reason whatsoever. The matters have clogged the docket for 8 years for no apparent reason to sustain the same. It appears that the applicant, Commissioner Inland Revenue, Zone II. LTU, Karachi, is not interested in proceeding with these matters. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, these reference applications are dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this order may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Office is instructed to place copy hereof in the connected files. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
